Class PlayerHand

java.lang.Object
com.example.elcincuentazo.model.PlayerHand
public class PlayerHand extends Object
Represents the set of cards currently held by one player.

A PlayerHand always starts empty and cards are added individually via addCard(Card). Cards may be removed when the player plays one or when the player is eliminated.

  • Field Summary

    Fields
    Modifier and Type
    Field
    Description
    static final int
    MAX_CARDS
    Maximum number of cards a player is allowed to hold at one time.

    Modifier and Type
    Method
    Description
    void
    addCard(Card card)
    Adds a card to this hand and marks it as being in a player's hand (location = 1).
    Card
    getCard(int index)
    Returns the card at the given index without removing it.
    List<Card>
    getCards()
    Returns an unmodifiable view of all cards currently in this hand.
    boolean
    isEmpty()
    Returns true if the hand contains no cards.
    Card
    removeCard(int index)
    Removes and returns the card at the specified index.
    boolean
    removeCard(Card card)
    Removes a specific card instance from the hand.
    int
    size()
    Returns the number of cards currently in this hand.
    String
    toString()
    Returns a display string listing all cards in this hand.

    • MAX_CARDS

      public static final int MAX_CARDS
      Maximum number of cards a player is allowed to hold at one time.

  • Constructor Details

    • PlayerHand

      public PlayerHand()
      Constructs an empty PlayerHand.

  • Method Details

    • addCard

      public void addCard(Card card)
      Adds a card to this hand and marks it as being in a player's hand (location = 1).
      Parameters:
      card - the Card to add; must not be null

    • removeCard

      public Card removeCard(int index)
      Removes and returns the card at the specified index.
      Parameters:
      index - zero-based index of the card to remove
      Returns:
      the removed Card
      Throws:
      IndexOutOfBoundsException - if the index is out of range

    • removeCard

      public boolean removeCard(Card card)
      Removes a specific card instance from the hand.
      Parameters:
      card - the card to remove
      Returns:
      true if the card was present and removed

    • getCard

      public Card getCard(int index)
      Returns the card at the given index without removing it.
      Parameters:
      index - zero-based index
      Returns:
      the Card at that position

    • getCards

      public List<Card> getCards()
      Returns an unmodifiable view of all cards currently in this hand.
      Returns:
      unmodifiable List of cards

    • size

      public int size()
      Returns the number of cards currently in this hand.
      Returns:
      card count

    • isEmpty

      public boolean isEmpty()
      Returns true if the hand contains no cards.
      Returns:
      true when empty

    • toString

      public String toString()
      Returns a display string listing all cards in this hand.
      Overrides:
      toString in class Object
      Returns:
      comma-separated card descriptions, or "(empty)" if none
